title: 控方证人
summary:
Dickie Carpenter recounts a series of unsettling encounters with a mysterious, beautiful woman named Alice Thurston Howarth, who is characterized by her golden hair and a red thin scarf. Dickie describes how, during a past social gathering, she spoke to him with a gentle, regretful tone, warning him, "If I were you, I wouldn't go in." He later encountered a woman resembling her, Mrs. Haversham, in the woods, who issued a similar warning. Most recently, at Dr. Johnny's private sanatorium, Dickie encountered a nurse in a red uniform whom he identified as Mrs. Howarth. She warned him against undergoing a surgery for his torpedo-injured leg, yet a nurse at the facility claimed no one by that name worked there.

McPherson later visits Alice Howarth at her home after obtaining her address from Loose. Upon his arrival, he hears a mournful song about a gypsy woman. Alice, described as possessing Scandinavian beauty, invites him to walk with her and her husband, Morris, toward the moor. During their walk, Alice reveals that Dickie died from anesthesia during a small operation. She dismisses the idea that she was the nurse who warned him, suggesting instead that a look-alike cousin was responsible. 

Alice claims to possess a "gift" or "curse" of second sight inherited from the Fergusson family and her medium mother, Kristin. She demonstrates this by prompting McPherson to look at holes in the rocks, which he perceives as being filled with blood. Alice explains that she married Morris to protect him from the terrible things haunting him, though she was unable to save Dickie.
